prompt: I enjoy exploring ideas that challenge the way I usually think.
I would rather repeat what already works than experiment with something new.
I make a clear plan before I begin something important.
I tend to leave important tasks until the last possible moment.
Being around other people usually gives me energy.
I would rather observe quietly than speak up early in a group.
When conflict happens, I try to understand the other person's side.
I find it easy to put my needs ahead of everyone else's.
Small setbacks can throw off my mood for longer than I would like.
I stay calm when plans suddenly change.
I like connecting ideas from very different fields just to see what happens.
Abstract or symbolic conversations usually feel less interesting than practical ones.
I feel better when priorities, deadlines, and next steps are clearly defined.
I often underestimate how much time a task will really take.
I naturally become more expressive when a room feels flat or quiet.
Too much social time drains me faster than it energizes me.
People usually experience me as patient, fair, and easy to work with.
When stakes are high, I can become blunt enough to ignore how others feel.
I replay stressful moments in my head longer than I want to.
Even in uncertainty, I can usually recover my balance quickly.
